name: pushsource

metadata.name: pushsource
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 or later (GPLv3+)
Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: A library to load push items from a variety of sources

documentation:
A Python library for collecting content from various sources, used by
[release-engineering](https://github.com/release-engineering) publishing tools.

[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pushsource)](https://pypi.org/project/pushsource/)
![Build Status](https://github.com/release-engineering/pushsource/actions/workflows/tox-test.yml/badge.svg)

- [Source](https://github.com/release-engineering/pushsource)
- [Documentation](https://release-engineering.github.io/pushsource/)


Installation
------------

Install the `pushsource` package from PyPI.

```
pip install pushsource
```


Usage Example
-------------

```python
from pushsource import Source

# Get a source of content; sources and their parameters can be
# specified by URL. This source will use a couple of RPMs from
# Fedora koji as the content source.
with Source.get('koji:https://koji.fedoraproject.org/kojihub?rpm=python3-3.7.5-2.fc31.x86_64.rpm,python3-3.7.5-2.fc31.src.rpm') as source:
  # Iterate over the content and do something with it:
  for push_item in source:
    publish(push_item)
```

Development
-----------

Patches may be contributed via pull requests to
https://github.com/release-engineering/pushsource.

All changes must pass the automated test suite, along with various static
checks.

The [Black](https://black.readthedocs.io/) code style is enforced.
Enabling autoformatting via a pre-commit hook is recommended:

```
pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
pre-commit install
```

License
-------

This program is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify
it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by
the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or
(at your option) any later version.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.validator.Validator

methods.name: pushsource._impl.validator.Validator.__init__
documentation:
Obtain a validator.

Arguments:

    schema_name (str)
        Name of schema within pushsource library to be used by
        this validator (e.g. "errata")

    ids (list[str])
        A list of attributes which can be used to identify user-meaningful
        objects in validated data.

        When validation fails, the innermost found object having any of these
        attributes will be considered the most relevant object to report to
        the user, and the attribute value will be included in a log message.

        Example: if validation fails on an object like this:

          {'object1': {'filename': 'foobar', 'metadata': {... FAILS HERE}}}

        ... and if 'ids' contains 'filename', then the validation failure
        will be logged as "foobar is not valid".

methods.name: pushsource._impl.validator.Validator.validate
documentation:
Validate an object against the configured schema.

This method behaves the same as `jsonschema.validate`, except that
if a validation error occurs, a user-oriented message is logged at
ERROR level before an exception is raised.

The idea is that a typical end-user has a better chance of understanding
a basic error message pointing at which piece of data failed validation,
than they do of understanding the jsonschema ValidationError in raw form.

Arguments:

    data (object)
        Any object to be validated.

    data_label (str)
        A label for the data being validated.

        This should be a brief string which would make sense to the end-user
        which will help identify the data failing validation.

        For example, if you are validating JSON loaded from a file,
        passing the filename as data_label would be reasonable - it will tell
        the user which file failed validation.

A helper to validate jsonschemas with some friendlier
log messages applied on validation errors.

modules.name: pushsource._impl.list_cmd
documentation:
List all push items found in one or more sources.

configuration:

  A simple configuration file may be provided at /etc/pushsource.conf and
  ~/.config/pushsource.conf in order to set up backends bound to a
  particular environment, as in example:

    # in ~/.config/pushsource.conf
    sources:
      # this will add a 'fedkoji' source, making it possible to do:
      # pushsource-ls fedkoji:rpm=python3-3.7.5-2.fc31.x86_64.rpm
      - name: fedkoji
        url: 'koji:https://koji.fedoraproject.org/kojihub'

see also:

  Information on available backends can be found at:
  https://release-engineering.github.io/pushsource/userguide.html

methods.name: pushsource._impl.list_cmd.ItemDumper.represent_enum
documentation: Custom dumper adding support for any types appearing on pushitems
which are not natively supported by pyyaml.

functions.name: pushsource._impl.helpers.list_argument
documentation:
Convert an argument into a list:

- if the value is already a list, it's returned verbatim
- if the value is None and retain_none is False, an empty list is returned
- if the value is None and retain_none is True, None is returned
- if the value is a string, it's split on ","
- otherwise, the value is wrapped in a list

This function is intended for use in {class}`~pushsource.Source` constructors
to ease the processing of arguments passed via URL.

Parameters:
: value

  : Any value.

Returns:
: list

  : `value` converted to or wrapped into a list.

  None

  : if input was None and retain_none is True.

functions.name: pushsource._impl.helpers.try_int
documentation:
Convert strings into integers where possible.

This function is intended for use in {class}`~pushsource.Source` constructors
to ease the processing of arguments passed via URL.

Parameters:
: value

  : Any value.

Returns:
: int

  : `value` converted to an integer, if it was a string representation
    of an integer.

  other

  : `value` unmodified, in any other case.

functions.name: pushsource._impl.helpers.try_bool
documentation:
Convert values into booleans where possible.

This function is intended for use in {class}`~pushsource.Source` constructors
to ease the processing of arguments passed via URL (which always arrive as a string).

Parameters:
: value

  : Any value.

Returns:
: bool

  : `value` converted to a boolean, if it was a string representation
    of a boolean.

  other

  : `value` unmodified, in any other case.

functions.name: pushsource._impl.helpers.force_https
documentation:
Force `url` to use https as its scheme.

Parameters:
: url: str

  : A string representation of a URL.

Returns:
: str

  : `url` with its scheme replaced with https.

Throws:

: Any potential error thrown by the urlparse function during parsing.

functions.name: pushsource._impl.helpers.as_completed_with_timeout_reset
documentation:
Yields any finished future within time alloted by timeout, raises TimeoutError otherwise.
Futures are allowed to continue over timeout, if any processing in caller is slow.
Timeout is not applied to futures themselves but rather to wait() call, which means
that this function allows to yield all quicker futures and tries to wait for slow ones.

Parameters:
    futures: List[Future]
        list of futures

    timeout: int or float
        Timeout used for wait() calls

Returns:
    Future
        finished Future object

Throws:
    TimeoutError, if waiting for some Future to finish in wait() call takes more
    time than given timeout.

functions.name: pushsource._impl.helpers.wait_exist
documentation:
Wait until a file begins to exist or until a timeout is reached.

Args:
    path (str):
        Path to check.
    timeout (int):
        Maximum time to wait.
    poll_rate (int):
        How often to check for file's existence.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.comps.CompsXmlPushItem
documentation:
A {class}`~pushsource.PushItem` representing a comps XML file.

For push items of this type, the {meth}`~pushsource.PushItem.src`
attribute refers to a file containing a comps XML file (package
group definitions) in the format used by yum & dnf.

This library does not verify that the referenced file is valid.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.ami.AmiSecurityGroup.from_port
documentation:
If the protocol is TCP or UDP, this is the start of the port range.
If the protocol is ICMP or ICMPv6, this is the type number.
A value of -1 indicates all ICMP/ICMPv6 types. If you specify all ICMP/ICMPv6 types,
you must specify all ICMP/ICMPv6 codes.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.ami.AmiSecurityGroup.ip_protocol

:type: list[str]

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.ami.AmiSecurityGroup.to_port

If the protocol is ICMP or ICMPv6, this is the code.
A value of -1 indicates all ICMP/ICMPv6 codes.
If you specify all ICMP/ICMPv6 types, you must specify all ICMP/ICMPv6 codes..

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.ami.AmiPushItem.marketplace_entity_type

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.ami.AmiPushItem.access_endpoint_url
documentation: Access endpoint url associated with this image.

A {class}`~pushsource.PushItem` representing an Amazon Machine Image (or "AMI").

For push items of this type, the {meth}`~pushsource.PushItem.src` attribute
refers to a file containing an EBS snapshot.  The push item contains additional
metadata which can be used to associate the snapshot with an image.

This library does not verify that the referenced file is a valid
snapshot.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.source.SourceUrlError
documentation:
Errors of this type are raised when an invalid URL is provided
to {meth}`~pushsource.Source.get` and related methods.

methods.name: pushsource._impl.source.SourceWrapper.__init__
documentation: Internal class to ensure that all source instances support enter/exit
for with statements even if underlying instance doesn't implement it
(since it originally was not mandatory).
This class also ensures that all source instances support polling
for the existence of source file before returning the push item.
This feature is disabled by default and can be enabled with
environment variables

methods.name: pushsource._impl.source.SourceWrapper.__iter__
documentation:
Poll until a source file is present before yielding an item.

This is necessary due to a limitation, where file changes may
not immediately propagate to NFS. If timeout is reached,
yield the item and display a warning.

Timeout and poll rate are controlled with environment variables.
PUSHSOURCE_SRC_POLL_TIMEOUT - Maximum number of seconds to poll for
PUSHSOURCE_SRC_POLL_RATE - How frequently to poll (in seconds)

The polling feature is disabled by default

Yields:
    PushItem:
        Created push item.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.source.Source

methods.name: pushsource._impl.source.Source.__iter__
documentation:
Iterate over the push items contained within this source.

Yields a series of {class}`~pushsource.PushItem` instances.

methods.name: pushsource._impl.source.Source.get
documentation:
Obtain a push source from the given URL.

Parameters:
: source_url (str)

  : Specifies a push source backend and associated arguments.
    For information about push source URLs, see
    {ref}`urls`.

  kwargs (dict)

  : Any additional keyword arguments to be passed into
    the backend.

Raises:
: SourceUrlError

  : If `source_url` is not a valid push source URL.

Returns:
: {class}`~pushsource.Source`

  : A new Source instance initialized with the given arguments.

methods.name: pushsource._impl.source.Source.get_partial
documentation:
Obtain a push source constructor from the given URL and arguments.

This method returns a constructor for a push source (rather than a push source
instance directly) so that additional arguments may be provided later.

One of the primary uses of this method is to provide your own preconfigured
aliases for existing backends, as described in {ref}`binding`.

Parameters:
: source_url (str)

  : Specifies a push source backend and associated arguments.
    For information about push source URLs, see
    {ref}`urls`.

  kwargs (dict)

  : Any additional keyword arguments to be passed into
    the backend.

Raises:
: SourceUrlError

  : If `source_url` is not a valid push source URL.

Returns:
: callable

  : A callable which accepts any number of keyword arguments
    and returns a {class}`~pushsource.Source`.

methods.name: pushsource._impl.source.Source.register_backend
documentation:
Register a new pushsource backend.

This method allows registering additional backends beyond those
shipped with the pushsource library. See {ref}`implementing` for
more information.

Parameters:
: name (str)

  : The name of a backend. This should be a brief unique identifying
    string.

    If a backend of the given name is already registered, it will be
    overwritten.

  factory (callable)

  : A callable used to create new instances of the backend.
    When invoked, this callable must return an object which implements
    the {class}`~pushsource.Source` interface.

Raises:
: TypeError

  : If `factory` is not callable.

methods.name: pushsource._impl.source.Source.reset
documentation:
Reset the library to the default configuration.

This method will undo the effect of any prior calls to
{meth}`~pushsource.Source.register_backend`, restoring only the
default backends provided by the pushsource library.

This may be used from within tests to ensure a known state.

:::{versionadded} 2.6.0
:::

A source of push items.

This base class defines the interface for all pushsource backends.
Instances of a specific backend can be obtained using
the {meth}`~pushsource.Source.get` method.

Though not mandatory, instances of `Source` are preferably used
via `with` statements to ensure that all resources are cleaned up
when no longer needed, as in example:

```python
with Source.get('some-url') as source:
    for item in source:
        do_something(item)
```

Note that the items produced by a source are not bound to the lifecycle
of a source instance, so it's safe to store them and continue using
them beyond the end of the `with` statement.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.file.FilePushItem

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.file.FilePushItem.description
documentation: A human-readable brief description of the file.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.file.FilePushItem.version
documentation:
A version string associated with the file.

This string is intended for display purposes only.
It may denote a product version associated with this file.
For example, a push item for `oc-4.2.33-linux.tar.gz` may
use a version of `"4.2.33"` to denote that the file relates
to OpenShift version 4.2.33.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.file.FilePushItem.display_order
documentation:
A display order hint associated with the file.

This value is intended for display purposes only.
In a UI presenting a file list, it is suggested that the default order
of files should be set by an ascending sort using this value.
This provides a method of keeping related files grouped together and
presented to users in a logical order.

:::{versionadded} 2.14.0
:::
A {class}`~pushsource.PushItem` representing a single generic file.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.directory.DirectoryPushItem
documentation:
A {class}`~pushsource.PushItem` representing a directory.

On a directory push item, the src attribute contains the full path to a directory tree.
It should generally be interpreted as a request to recursively publish that entire directory
tree as-is.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.azure.VHDPushItem

methods.name: pushsource._impl.model.azure.VHDPushItem._from_data
documentation: Instantiate VHDPushItem from raw list or dict

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.azure.VHDPushItem.generation
documentation: VHD generation. It can be `V1` or `V2`.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.azure.VHDPushItem.sku_id
documentation: SKU ID for the VM image generation, generally being the Plan ID.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.azure.VHDPushItem.support_legacy
documentation: If the image `generation == V2` but also supports `V1`.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.azure.VHDPushItem.legacy_sku_id
documentation: The `V1` SKU ID (only if `support_legacy` is true).

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.azure.VHDPushItem.disk_version
documentation: The image's disk version according to Azure's format {int}.{int}.{int}

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.azure.VHDPushItem.recommended_sizes
documentation: List of recommended VM sizes in Azure, for example ['a1-standard', 'a5', 'a7']

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.azure.VHDPushItem.sas_uri
documentation: The SAS URI to be associated with the disk_version.

A {class}`~pushsource.PushItem` representing a Virtual Hard Disk (or VHD).

For push items of this type, the {meth}`~pushsource.PushItem.src` attribute
refers to a file containing the VHD file..

This library does not verify that the referenced file is a valid
snapshot.

Note: As some attributes can not be obtained directly from Koji they're marked
as optional in the `attrs` validators. With this the VHDPushItem objects can be
created without these attributes and the missing values can be added later by
using `attrs.evolve`.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.rpm.RpmPushItem

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.rpm.RpmPushItem.module_build
documentation:
NVR for the koji module build through which this push item was located, if any.

For a modular RPM push item, there may be two koji builds in play:

`module_build` (this attribute):

: NVR of a build which produced modulemd YAML files referencing this RPM
  (but the build itself does not contain RPMs).

  Example: [ghc-8.4-820200708061905.9edba152]
  is a valid `module_build` for `ghc-8.4.4-74.module_el8+12161+cf1bd7f2.x86_64.rpm`.

{meth}`~pushsource.PushItem.build`:

: NVR of the build containing the RPM.

  Example: [ghc-8.4.4-74.module_el8+12161+cf1bd7f2]
  is a valid build for `ghc-8.4.4-74.module_el8+12161+cf1bd7f2.x86_64.rpm`.

Note that the `module_build` itself isn't a property of the RPM but rather should
be considered contextual information on how any given RPM was located. The same RPM
may have different values of `module_build` when loaded from different contexts.

[ghc-8.4-820200708061905.9edba152]: https://koji.fedoraproject.org/koji/buildinfo?buildID=1767200
[ghc-8.4.4-74.module_el8+12161+cf1bd7f2]: https://koji.fedoraproject.org/koji/buildinfo?buildID=1767130
A {class}`~pushsource.PushItem` representing a single RPM.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.cache.TinyCache

methods.name: pushsource._impl.model.cache.TinyCache.__init__
documentation:
Construct a cache.

Arguments:
    cache_type
        Type(s) used for an isinstance() check.

        Only values of this type are eligible for caching;
        anything else will be returned as-is.

    converter
        A callable to convert input values before caching.

        TinyCache is meant to be used as an attrs field converter.
        This argument can be used to chain onto another converter
        where needed.

A tiny specialized cache capable of reusing (very) recent values.

These caches are meant to be used as attr field converters, with one
cache created per cacheable field.

Whenever a new value for that field is about to be saved, the cache
will first be consulted. If the new value is equal to the last couple
of values for the same field (across all constructed objects), then
the value from cache will be returned instead.

The caching strategy may seem odd, but it works out well due to the
way push items tend to be constructed in practice. For example: it
is common for every RPM in a push to use the same signing key. In
that scenario, this cache will allow every signing_key field to
reference exactly the same string rather than a copy.
Similarly, it is common to construct many items having the same
'dest'.

Compared to a more conventional cache storing some values in a list
or dict, this has the advantage that the cache size is always fixed
and very small, meaning it is very unlikely that the cache overhead
can exceed the savings from using the cache.

Here are some measurements of the impact of this caching on a real
data set of ~52,000 push items:

+------------------+-----------+---------------+
| case             | mem (KiB) | total objects |
+==================+===========+===============+
| no cache (min)   | 103928    | 261182        |
| no cache (max)   | 120096    | 261420        |
| tiny cache (min) | 98472     | 252582        |
| tiny cache (max) | 109608    | 252466        |
+------------------+-----------+---------------+

We create about 10,000 less python objects in total and reduce
push item memory usage between 5% .. 13%.

It is of course only safe for use on immutable types.

Returns a list of ProductIDs described by the ProductID X.509 certificate file
in PEM format. Raises ValueError if the file doesn't describe any ProductID.

List of products described by the ProductID certificate.

```

List[ProductID]
```

:::

A {class}`~pushsource.PushItem` representing a product ID certificate.

For push items of this type, the {meth}`~pushsource.PushItem.src` attribute
refers to a file containing a PEM certificate identifying a product.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumReference

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumReference.href
documentation: A URL.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumReference.id
documentation: A short ID for the reference, unique within this erratum.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumReference.title
documentation:
A title for the reference; analogous to the 'title' attribute
in HTML.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumReference.type
documentation:
Type of reference. This defines the expected target of the URL
and includes at least:

- "self": reference to a page for this advisory
- "bugzilla": reference to a bug
- "other": any other kind of reference
A reference within a {meth}`~ErratumPushItem.references` list.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumModule

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumModule.name
documentation: Module name.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumModule.stream
documentation: Module stream.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumModule.version
documentation: Module version.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumModule.context
documentation: Module context.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumModule.arch
documentation: Module architecture.
A module entry within a {meth}`~ErratumPushItem.pkglist`.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPackage

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPackage.arch
documentation: RPM architecture.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPackage.filename
documentation: RPM filename (basename).

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPackage.epoch
documentation: RPM epoch.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPackage.name
documentation: RPM name (e.g. "bash-4.0.1-1.el7.x86_64.rpm" name is "bash")

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPackage.version
documentation: RPM version (e.g. "bash-4.0.1-1.el7.x86_64.rpm" version is "4.0.1")

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPackage.release
documentation: RPM release (e.g. "bash-4.0.1-1.el7.x86_64.rpm" version is "1.el7")

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPackage.src
documentation:
Filename of the source RPM from which this RPM was built; equal to
{meth}`filename` for the source RPM itself.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPackage.reboot_suggested
documentation: True if rebooting host machine is recommended after installing this package.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPackage.md5sum
documentation: MD5 checksum of this RPM in hex string form, if available.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPackage.sha1sum
documentation: SHA1 checksum of this RPM in hex string form, if available.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPackage.sha256sum
documentation: SHA256 checksum of this RPM in hex string form, if available.
A package (RPM) entry within a {meth}`~ErratumPushItem.pkglist`.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPackageCollection

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPackageCollection.name
documentation:
A name for this collection. The collection name has no specific meaning,
but must be unique within an advisory.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPackageCollection.packages
documentation:
List of packages within this collection.

:type: list[ErratumPackage]

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPackageCollection.short
documentation:
An alternative name for this collection. In practice, this field
is typically blank.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPackageCollection.module
documentation:
An {class}`~ErratumModule` defining the module this entry is associated
with, if any.

A collection of packages found within an {meth}`~ErratumPushItem.pkglist`.

A non-modular advisory typically contains only a single collection, while modular
advisories typically contain one collection per module.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPushItem

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPushItem.type
documentation: 'bugfix', 'security' or 'enhancement'.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPushItem.release
documentation: Release number. Typically an integer-string, initially '0'.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPushItem.status
documentation: Status, typically 'final'.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPushItem.pushcount
documentation: Number of times advisory has been revised and published (starting at '1').

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPushItem.reboot_suggested
documentation:
True if rebooting host machine is recommended after installing this advisory.

:::{warning}
The intended usage of this field is unclear.

In practice, tools such as yum are instead consuming the `reboot_suggested` field
from {class}`ErratumPackage`.
:::

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPushItem.references
documentation:
A list of references associated with the advisory.

:type: list[ErratumReference]

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPushItem.pkglist
documentation:
A list of package collections associated with the advisory.

:type: list[ErratumPackageCollection]

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPushItem.container_list
pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPushItem.from_
documentation:
Contact email address for the owner of the advisory.

Note that the canonical name for this attribute is `from`. As this clashes
with a Python keyword, in most contexts the attribute is also available as an
alias, `from_`. Where possible, the canonical name `from` should be preferred.

```
type

str
```

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPushItem.rights
documentation: Copyright message.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPushItem.title
documentation: Title of the advisory (e.g. 'bash bugfix and enhancement').

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPushItem.description
documentation: Full human-readable description of the advisory, usually multiple lines.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPushItem.version
documentation:
Advisory version. Starts counting at "1", and some systems require updating
the version whenever an advisory is modified.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPushItem.updated
documentation:
Timestamp of the last update to this advisory.

Typically of the form '2019-12-31 06:54:41 UTC', but this is not enforced.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPushItem.issued
documentation:
Timestamp of the initial release of this advisory.

Uses the same format as {meth}`updated`.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPushItem.severity
documentation: Severity of the advisory, e.g. "low", "moderate", "important" or "critical".

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPushItem.summary
documentation: Typically a single sentence briefly describing the advisory.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPushItem.solution
documentation: Text explaining how to apply the advisory.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum.ErratumPushItem.content_types
documentation:
A list of content types associated with the advisory.

For example, "docker" may be found in this list if the advisory deals
with container images.

:type: list[str]

A {class}`~pushsource.PushItem` representing a single erratum (also known as "advisory").

Note that many fields on erratum objects which appear to be numeric
are instead represented as strings ('release' and 'pushcount' being two
examples).

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.modulemd.ModuleMdPushItem
documentation:
A {class}`~pushsource.PushItem` representing a modulemd stream.

For push items of this type, the {meth}`~pushsource.PushItem.src` attribute
refers to a file containing a YAML document stream. The stream is expected
to contain one or more modulemd or modulemd-defaults documents.

This library does not verify that the referenced file is a valid
modulemd stream.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.modulemd.ModuleMdSourcePushItem
documentation:
A {class}`~pushsource.PushItem` representing a modulemd source/packager document.

Similar to {class}`~pushsource.ModuleMdPushItem`, but refers to the source
document, typically named `modulemd.src.txt` in koji.

This library does not verify that the referenced file is a valid
modulemd source document.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.cgw.CGWPushItem
documentation:
A {class}`~pushsource.PushItem` representing a Content Gateway (CGW) YAML file.

For push items of this type, the {meth}`~pushsource.PushItem.src`
attribute refers to a YAML file containing list of definitions
for objects in Content Gateway.

This library does not verify that the referenced YAML file is valid.

modules.name: pushsource._impl.model.conv
documentation: Converters and validators for use with model classes.

functions.name: pushsource._impl.model.conv.sloppylist
documentation:
Accept real lists or comma-separated values, and output a frozen list.

Optionally use elem_converter to convert each list element.

functions.name: pushsource._impl.model.conv.archstr
documentation: Convert a handful of arch aliases into canonical form for consistent comparisons.

functions.name: pushsource._impl.model.conv.freeze
documentation:
Convert complex object composed of dicts and lists to equivalent object composed of
frozendicts and frozenlists.

functions.name: pushsource._impl.model.conv.unfreeze
documentation:
Convert complex object composed of frozendicts and frozenlists to equivalent object composed of
dicts and lists.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.base.KojiBuildInfo

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.base.KojiBuildInfo.name
documentation:
'name' component of build's NVR.

Example: in "kf5-kio-5.83.0-2.el8.next", the name is "kf5-kio".

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.base.KojiBuildInfo.version
documentation:
'version' component of build's NVR.

Example: in "kf5-kio-5.83.0-2.el8.next", the version is "5.83.0".

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.base.KojiBuildInfo.release
documentation:
'release' component of build's NVR.

Example: in "kf5-kio-5.83.0-2.el8.next", the release is "2.el8.next".

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.base.KojiBuildInfo.id
documentation: Optional attribute to store the 'build_id' from Koji.
A representation of a koji build.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.base.PushItem

methods.name: pushsource._impl.model.base.PushItem.with_checksums
documentation:
Return a copy of this push item with checksums present.

Many pushsource backends will produce push items with empty checksum attributes,
since checksum calculation may be expensive and may not be required in all cases.

The rule of thumb is that a pushsource will only provide checksums if they were
available in metadata, so that reading the push item's file was unnecessary.
For example:

- with `errata` push source, checksums are generally available by default, because
  Errata Tool itself provides checksums on the files contained in an advisory.
- with `staged` push source, checksums are unavailable by default, because the
  sums can only be calculated by reading file content from the staging area.

Where checksums are needed, this utility method may be used to ensure they are present
regardless of the push source used.

This method entails reading the entire content of the file referenced
by this push item, and may be:

- slow: may need to read a large file from a remote system, e.g. from an NFS volume.
- error-prone: reads from a remote system might fail.

As such, when dealing with a large number of push items, you may want to consider
using multiple threads to parallelize calls to `with_checksums`, and retrying
failing operations.

If checksums are already present or if this item does not reference a file, this
method is a no-op and returns the current push item, unmodified.

Returns:
: {class}`~pushsource.PushItem`

  : A copy of this item, guaranteed either to have non-empty {meth}`md5sum` and
    {meth}`sha256sum` attributes, or an empty {meth}`src` attribute (denoting that
    the item does not reference a file).

:::{versionadded} 1.2.0
:::

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.base.PushItem.name
documentation:
A name for this push item.

A push item's name may be any short identifying string which is meaningful
to end-users.

In the common case of a regular file, a push item name will simply be a filename,
optionally including leading path components.

In all cases, a non-empty name must be provided.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.base.PushItem.state
documentation:
The state of this push item.

In the majority of cases, push sources will produce push items with
a state of `PENDING`, with the caller expected to later evolve the state.

In rare cases, push sources may produce push items with other states, such
as `INVALIDFILE` to indicate that the source located invalid content.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.base.PushItem.src
documentation:
The source of this push item.

If the push item is a file, this will be the full path to the file.
If the push item does not represent a file, this will generally be omitted.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.base.PushItem.dest
documentation:
Destination of this push item.

The meaning of "dest" differs depending on the source used and its configuration.

Examples for possible uses of "dest" include:

* a path to a directory (for items pushed using rsync)
* a Pulp repository name (for items pushed using Pulp)

:type: list[str]

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.base.PushItem.md5sum
documentation:
Hex digest of MD5 checksum for this push item, if available.

:::{seealso}
{meth}`with_checksums`
:::

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.base.PushItem.sha256sum
documentation:
Hex digest of SHA256 checksum for this push item, if available.

:::{seealso}
{meth}`with_checksums`
:::

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.base.PushItem.origin
documentation:
A string representing the origin of this push item.

The "origin" field is expected to record some info on how this push item
was discovered. The exact semantics depend on the specific source backend
in use.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.base.PushItem.build
documentation:
NVR for the koji build from which this push item was extracted, if any.

:::{seealso}
{meth}`build_info`, which provides the same info in parsed form.
:::

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.base.PushItem.build_info
documentation: Basic info on the koji build from which this push item was extracted, if any.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.base.PushItem.signing_key
documentation:
If this push item was GPG signed, this should be an identifier for the
signing key used.

Generally a short key ID such as "F21541EB" is used, though the library
doesn't enforce this.

A PushItem represents a single piece of content to be published.
This may be an RPM, an advisory, a generic file, and so on.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.BootMode

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.BootMode.hybrid
documentation: Supports both UEFI and BIOS mode.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.BootMode.uefi
documentation: Supports only UEFI mode.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.BootMode.legacy
documentation: Supports only BIOS mode.
Represent the possible boot modes for VM images.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMICloudInfo

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMICloudInfo.provider
documentation: The cloud provider's name, e.g.: "aws".

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMICloudInfo.account
documentation: The cloud provider's account alias, e.g.: "aws-na".

Information on the cloud provider associated with a given push item.

Cloud provider information is only available for VMIs which have previously
been published to a cloud. It may be used to locate an existing VMI for
manipulation, such as metadata updates or deletion.

This library doesn't define any specific cloud provider names or aliases.
Generally, a user of this library is expected to use the information here
to look up cloud access details from a configuration file or other source.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMIRelease

methods.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMIRelease._from_data
documentation: Instantiate VMIRelease from raw dict

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMIRelease.product
documentation:
A short product name, for example "RHEL" if this is an image for Red Hat
Enterprise Linux.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMIRelease.date
documentation: Date at which this image was generated.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMIRelease.arch
documentation: Architecture of the image, for example "x86_64".

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMIRelease.respin
documentation:
Respin count. 0 for original build of an image, incremented by one for
each rebuild.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMIRelease.version
documentation:
A <major>.<minor> version string for the image's product version, for example
"7.9" if this is an image for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7.9.x.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMIRelease.base_product
documentation: For layered products, name of the base product for which the image should be used.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMIRelease.base_version
documentation: For layered products, version of the base product for which the image should be used.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMIRelease.variant
documentation:
Variant of this image's product (only for products which have variants).  For example,
"Server", for Red Hat Enterprise Linux Server.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMIRelease.type
documentation: Release type, typically "ga" or "beta".
Release metadata associated with a VM image.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMIPushItem

methods.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMIPushItem.marketplace_title
documentation:
The marketplace title which is used for some certain layered products.

It's built from the `marketplace_title_template` by formatting it with the proper values.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMIPushItem._RELEASE_TYPE
documentation: The expected release type for this class.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMIPushItem.release
documentation: Release metadata associated with this image.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMIPushItem.description
documentation: A brief human-readable description of the image.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMIPushItem.boot_mode
documentation: Boot mode supported by the image (if known): uefi, legacy, or hybrid (uefi + legacy).

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMIPushItem.cloud_info
documentation: Cloud provider information, such as the provider's short name and account alias.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMIPushItem.marketplace_title_template
documentation:
The template is of the form used by `str.format`, with available keywords being all of
the documented fields on `VMIRelease` and `AMIRelease` classes.

It's used by the property `marketplace_title` to format it as the marketplace title.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.vms.VMIPushItem.marketplace_name
documentation: Name of the marketplace where the Image is expected to be shipped.

A {class}`~pushsource.PushItem` representing a generic VM image.

This class is used as a parent class for different types of VM images.

modules.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum_fixup
documentation:
Helpers to deal with "from" vs "from_" issue in ErratumPushItem.

The advisory model has a "from" field, which unfortunately clashes with
a Python keyword of the same name. This prevents directly declaring the
field as "from".

Despite the clash, it is desirable to have the attrs field named "from"
so that usage of this model does not have to be frequently accompanied
by some "from_" => "from" renaming (e.g. before uploading to Pulp).

It is possible to make this work by:

- in the ErratumPushItem class, declare the attribute as "from_"
- then, after the class is created, patch it to effectively rename it
  to "from"

This makes use of the __attrs_attrs__ attribute which is associated
with each attrs class. Though the name implies it is private, this is
a supported & documented method of extending the behavior of attrs,
see: https://www.attrs.org/en/stable/extending.html

A simpler trick may come to mind: just setattr(ErratumPushItem, 'from', attr.ib(...))
to work around the inability to write "from = attr.ib(...)".
Unfortunately, that does not work because the attrs library internally
tries to eval some code of the form "<attr_name> = ...", which will never
work if the name is a keyword.

methods.name: pushsource._impl.model.erratum_fixup.AttrsRenamer.__init__
documentation:
Helper to selectively rename certain attributes for __attrs_attrs__
within a class.

Arguments:
    delegate (tuple)
        Original instance of __attrs_attrs__, generated by attrs library.
    attrs_old_to_new (dict)
        A mapping from old to new attribute names.

methods.name: pushsource._impl.backend.direct.DirectSource.__init__
documentation: A source which directly constructs a single item from given arguments.
For example:
- "file:/some/file.txt"
- will yield a FilePushItem(name="file.txt", src="/some/file.txt")
Note that this class is not public API. Instances of this source can
be obtained only by URL, using the names registered toward the end
of this file.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.backend.staged.staged_source.StagedSource

methods.name: pushsource._impl.backend.staged.staged_source.StagedSource.__init__
documentation:
Create a new source.

Parameters:
: url (list\[str\])

  : URL(s) of locally accessible staging directories, e.g.
    `"/mnt/staging/my-content-for-push"`.

    These directories must follow the documented layout for staging areas.

  threads (int)

  : Number of threads used for concurrent loading of files.

  timeout (int)

  : Number of seconds after which an error is raised, if no progress is
    made during each step.

Uses a directory with a predefined layout (a "staging directory") as
the source of push items.

modules.name: pushsource._impl.backend.broken_rpmlib
documentation: A replacement for kobo.rpmlib to give a decent error message
when rpm bindings are not available.
importer is expected to put a more specific cause here.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImagePullSpec

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImagePullSpec.registry
documentation:
Registry component of pull spec.

Example: in `"registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-minimal:latest"`,
the registry is `"registry.access.redhat.com"`.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImagePullSpec.repository
documentation:
Repository component of pull spec.

Example: in `"registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-minimal:latest"`,
the repository is `"ubi8/ubi-minimal"`.

A container image pull spec in parsed form.

Pull spec refers to a `"NAME[:TAG|@DIGEST]"` string used to pull
a container image. This class provides a pull spec both in raw
string form and parsed into various components, along with information
on the available media type.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImageTagPullSpec

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImageTagPullSpec.tag
documentation:
Tag component of pull spec.

Example: in `"registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-minimal:latest"`,
the tag is `"latest"`.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImageTagPullSpec.media_types
documentation:
Media type(s) expected to be reachable via this tag, or empty
if unknown.

Generally, the media types are only known for manifest lists obtained
from a koji build.

Example: `["application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json"]`

```
type

List[str]
```
A container image pull spec using a tag.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImageDigestPullSpec

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImageDigestPullSpec.digest
documentation:
Digest component of pull spec.

Example: in `"registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-minimal@sha256:0ccb9988abbc72d383258d58a7f519a10b637d472f28fbca6eb5fab79ba82a6b"`,
the digest is `"sha256:0ccb9988abbc72d383258d58a7f519a10b637d472f28fbca6eb5fab79ba82a6b"`.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImageDigestPullSpec.media_type
documentation:
Media type of the manifest obtained by this pull spec, if known.

Generally, the media type is known for all digest pull specs obtained
via koji. It can be checked to determine whether a pull spec refers to
a manifest list or an image manifest.

Example: ``"application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json"``
A container image pull spec using a manifest digest.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImagePullInfo

methods.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImagePullInfo.digest_spec_for_type
documentation:
Get the digest spec for a specific media type, if available.

Parameters:
: media_type (str)

  : a MIME type string identifying a type of image manifest or
    manifest list, e.g. "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json"

Returns:
: {class}`ContainerImageDigestPullSpec`

  : if a digest of the requested type is available

  `None`

  : if a digest of the requested type is not available

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImagePullInfo.tag_specs
documentation:
Pull specs to access this image by tag.

This may include specs which refer to a *manifest list* referencing this
image, and also specs which refer directly to the *image manifest* for
this image.

This field always contains at least one pull spec. If you don't care
which tag you use, it's reasonable to use `item.pull_info.tag_specs[0]`.

Note that, as all tags are mutable, it's possible for this information to
be outdated. It is recommended to pull by digest or at least
cross-reference with digests.

```
type

List[ContainerImageTagPullSpec]
```

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImagePullInfo.digest_specs
documentation:
Pull specs to access this image by manifest digest.

This may include specs which refer to a *manifest list* referencing this
image, and also specs which refer directly to the *image manifest* for
this image.

These specs are always ordered from most to least preferred manifest type,
which means:

- manifest list if available, else:
- schema2 image manifest if available, else:
- schema1 image manifest

:type: List[ContainerImageDigestPullSpec]

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImagePullInfo.media_types
documentation:
All media types for which a manifest is reachable in {meth}`digest_specs`.

For information on these types see the [image manifest spec].

```
type

List[str]
```

[image manifest spec]: http://web.archive.org/web/20210614172014/https://docs.docker.com/registry/spec/manifest-v2-2/#media-types
Information needed to access a container image from a registry.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImagePushItem

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImagePushItem.dest_signing_key
documentation:
Desired signing key for this container image.

Typically a 16-character identifier for a signing key, such
as `"199e2f91fd431d51"`, though the format is not enforced
by this library.

A non-empty value for this attribute should be interpreted as a request to
use the specified key to generate signatures for this image's manifest(s).
See [container-signature] docs for information on signatures.

[container-signature]: https://github.com/containers/image/blob/33bcba75bb181318608f989e18e086f0d83d254c/docs/containers-signature.5.md

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImagePushItem.source_tags
documentation:
Tags placed onto this image when it was built, if known.

:type: List[str]

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImagePushItem.labels
documentation:
Labels of this image, if known.

This field is not guaranteed to include all labels associated with the image.

:type: Dict[str, str]

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImagePushItem.arch
documentation:
Architecture of this image, if known.

This field uses the conventional architecture strings used throughout the
container ecosystem, such as `"amd64"`.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImagePushItem.pull_info
documentation: Metadata for pulling this image from a registry.

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.ContainerImagePushItem.product_name
documentation:
Name of the product of this image.

Brew doesn't provide this information, so it may not be set if the push
items are only generated from brew. This information will be included in the
"container security manifests" (formerly known as SBOMs).

A {class}`~pushsource.PushItem` representing a container image.

This item represents any kind of image which can be pulled
from a container image registry (i.e. an object identified by a
`NAME[:TAG|@DIGEST]` string).

In the case of a multi-arch image, one ContainerImagePushItem represents
an image for a single architecture only.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.SourceContainerImagePushItem
documentation:
A {class}`~pushsource.PushItem` representing a source container image.

Source container images are a special type of image which are not runnable
but instead contain the packaged source code of a related binary image.

See [this article](https://access.redhat.com/articles/3410171) for more
information on source container images.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.OperatorManifestPushItem

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.OperatorManifestPushItem.related_images
documentation:
Operator related images obtained from a koji build.

:type: List[str]

class_variables.name: pushsource._impl.model.container.OperatorManifestPushItem.container_image_items
documentation:
List of related container image push items.

:type: List[ContainerImagePushItem]

A {class}`~pushsource.PushItem` representing an operator manifests archive
(typically named `operator_manifests.zip`).

modules.name: pushsource._impl.backend.modulemd
documentation: Utilities for dealing with modulemd files from backends.
None of this is public API.

modules.name: pushsource._impl.backend.koji_containers
documentation: Helpers for dealing with container metadata from koji.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.backend.registry_source.RegistrySource

methods.name: pushsource._impl.backend.registry_source.RegistrySource.__init__
documentation:
Create a new source.

Parameters:
: image (str, list\[str\])

  : Pull spec(s) of container images, as a list or a comma-separated string.

    Each pull spec must include a hostname and a tag; referencing images by digest
    is currently not supported.

    `registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi:8.4-211` is an example of a valid pull
    spec.

  dest (str, list\[str\])

  : If provided, this value will be used to populate {meth}`~pushsource.PushItem.dest`
    on generated push items.

  dest_signing_key (str, list\[str\])

  : If provided, this value will be used to populate
    {meth}`~pushsource.ContainerImagePushItem.dest_signing_key` on generated
    push items.

    Note that, as each item holds only a single `dest_signing_key`, using this
    argument can affect the number of generated push items. For example,
    providing two keys would produce double the amount of push items as providing
    a single key.

  product_name (str)

  : If provided, this value will be used to populate
    {meth}`~pushsource.ContainerImagePushItem.product_name` on generated push items.
Uses a container image registry as a source of push items.

methods.name: pushsource._impl.backend.pub_source.pub_client.PubClient.get_json_f
documentation:
Returns Future[dict|list] holding json obj with AMI or VHD
push items returned from Pub for given task id.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.backend.errata_source.errata_client.ErrataClientBase

methods.name: pushsource._impl.backend.errata_source.errata_client.ErrataClientBase.get_raw_f
documentation: Returns Future[ErrataRaw] holding all ET responses for a particular advisory.
pylint: disable=W0223

methods.name: pushsource._impl.backend.errata_source.errata_client.ErrataHTTPClient.authenticate
documentation:
Use the keytab to create a Kerberos ticket granting ticket.

This method is expected to be called before any HTTP queries to errata are made.

methods.name: pushsource._impl.backend.errata_source.errata_client.ErrataHTTPClient._errata_service
documentation:
Create requests Session.

Session is used so that Kerberos authentication only has to be done once.
As pushsource utilizes threading, and sessions are not thread safe, each thread
will have a separate session.

Returns (object):
    Authenticated requests Session object.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.backend.pub_source.pub_source.PubSource

methods.name: pushsource._impl.backend.pub_source.pub_source.PubSource.__init__
documentation:
Create a new source.

Parameters:
    url (src)
        Base URL of Pub Tool, e.g. "http://pub.example.com",
        "https://pub.example.com:8123".

    task_id (int, str, list[str], list[int])
        Task ID(s) to be used as push item source.
        If a single string is given, multiple IDs may be
        comma-separated.

    threads (int)
        Number of threads used for concurrent queries to Pub.

    timeout (int)
        Number of seconds after which an error is raised, if no progress is
        made during queries to Pub.
Uses push item list from Pub as the source of push items.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.backend.koji_source.KojiSource

methods.name: pushsource._impl.backend.koji_source.KojiSource.__init__
documentation:
Create a new source.

Parameters:
: url (str)

  : URL of the XML-RPC endpoint for koji hub.

  dest (str, list\[str\])

  : The destination(s) to fill in for push items created by this
    source. If omitted, all push items have empty destinations.

  rpm (list\[str, int\])

  : RPM identifier(s). Can be koji IDs (integers) or filenames.
    The source will yield all RPMs identified by this list.

  module_build (list\[str, int\])

  : Build identifier(s). Can be koji IDs (integers) or build NVRs.
    The source will yield modulemd files belonging to these
    build(s), filtered by `module_filter_filename` (if given).

  module_filter_filename (list\[str\])

  : Filename(s) of modulemd archives to include in output.

    When `module_build` is given, modulemd files are located as
    koji archives with names of the form `modulemd.<arch>.txt`.
    By default, all of these archives will be processed.

    Providing a non-empty list for `module_filter_filename` will
    instead only process modulemd files with those filenames. This
    can be used to select only certain arches.

    Has no effect if `module_build` is not provided.

  container_build (list\[str, int\])

  : Build identifier(s). Can be koji IDs (integers) or build NVRs.
    The source will yield all container images produced by these
    build(s), along with associated artifacts such as operator manifest
    bundles.

  vmi_build (list\[str, int\])

  : Virtual Machine Image identifier(s). Can be koji build IDs (integers)
    or build NVRs.
    The source will yield all VMIs identified by this list.

  signing_key (list\[str\])

  : GPG signing key ID(s). If provided, content must be signed
    using one of the provided keys. Include `None` if unsigned
    should also be permitted.

    Keys should be listed in the order of preference.

  basedir (str)

  : Base directory of koji content (e.g. /mnt/koji).
    This directory must be readable locally.

  threads (int)

  : Number of threads used for concurrent queries to koji.

  timeout (int)

  : Number of seconds after which an error is raised, if no progress is
    made during queries to koji.

  cache (dict)

  : A cache used to retain the results of XML-RPC calls, to avoid
    repeated calls.

    Providing a cache only gives a benefit if the current process
    will create more than a single instance of KojiSource. In this
    case, some calls may be avoided by passing the same cache
    to each instance.

  executor (concurrent.futures.Executor)

  : A custom executor used to submit calls to koji.
Uses koji artifacts as the source of push items.

classes.name: pushsource._impl.backend.errata_source.errata_source.ErrataSource

methods.name: pushsource._impl.backend.errata_source.errata_source.ErrataSource.__init__
documentation:
Create a new source.

Parameters:
: url (src)

  : Base URL of Errata Tool, e.g. "<http://errata.example.com>",
    "<https://errata.example.com:8123>".

  errata (str, list\[str\])

  : Advisory ID(s) to be used as push item source.
    If a single string is given, multiple IDs may be
    comma-separated.

  koji_source (str)

  : URL of a koji source associated with this Errata Tool
    instance.

  rpm_filter_arch (str, list\[str\])

  : If provided, only RPMs for these given arch(es) will be produced;
    e.g. "x86_64", "src" and "noarch".

  legacy_container_repos (bool)

  : If `True`, any container push items generated by this source will
    use a legacy format for repository IDs.

    This is intended to better support certain legacy code and will be
    removed when no longer needed. Only use this if you know that you
    need it.

  keytab_path (str):

  : Kerberos keytab path for authenticating with Errata HTTP API.

  principal (str):

  : Kerberos principal for authenticating with Errata HTTP API.

  threads (int)

  : Number of threads used for concurrent queries to Errata Tool
    and koji.

  timeout (int)

  : Number of seconds after which an error is raised, if no progress is
    made during queries to Errata Tool.
Uses an advisory from Errata Tool as the source of push items.

functions.name: pushsource._impl.utils.containers.request.update_auth_header
documentation:
Adds the token into the request's headers as specified in the Docker v2 API documentation.
https://docs.docker.com/registry/spec/auth/token/#using-the-bearer-token

functions.name: pushsource._impl.utils.containers.request.request_token
documentation:
Attempts to retrieve the correct token based on the 401 response header.
According to the Docker API v2 documentation, the token be retrieved by issuing a GET
request to the url specified by the `realm` within the `WWW-Authenticate` header. This
request should add the following query parameters:
    service: the name of the service that hosts the desired resource
    scope:   the specific resource and permissions requested
https://docs.docker.com/registry/spec/auth/token/#requesting-a-token

functions.name: pushsource._impl.utils.containers.request.parse_401_response_headers
documentation:
Parse the headers from a 401 response into a dictionary that contains the information
necessary to retrieve a token.
Example:
Www-Authenticate: Bearer realm="https://auth.docker.io/token",
service="registry.docker.io",scope="repository:library/nginx:pull,push"

functions.name: pushsource._impl.utils.containers.request.registry_request
documentation:
Retrieve a single path within the upstream registry, and return a
2-tuple of the headers and the response body.

functions.name: pushsource._impl.utils.containers.request._calculate_digest
documentation: Copied from https://github.com/pulp/pulp_docker.git

functions.name: pushsource._impl.utils.containers.request.api_version_check
documentation:
Make a call to the registry URL's /v2/ API call to determine if the registry supports API
v2.
